title: Dryopsis ferruginea
intro: Dryopsis ferruginea, also known as the rusty club fern, is a species of fern native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopsis ferruginea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Dryopsis ferruginea is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and teas for respiratory and inflammatory issues.
taxonomy: Dryopsis ferruginea is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to North America. The classification of Dryopsis ferruginea is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Polypodiales, family - Dryopteridaceae, genus - Dryopsis, species - D. ferruginea. This classification suggests that Dryopsis ferruginea is a terrestrial fern with fern-like foliage. The family Dryopteridaceae, to which Dryopsis ferruginea belongs, is a group of mostly terrestrial ferns.
